question_text: Two objects have the same volume but different masses. Object A has a mass of 50 g and Object B has a mass of 100 g. Which object has the greater density and why?
correct_answer: Object B has greater density because it has twice the mass in the same volume, so it has more mass per unit volume.
explanation: Since density = mass ÷ volume, and both objects have the same volume, the object with the greater mass must have the greater density. Object B (100 g) is heavier than Object A (50 g) in the same space, so it is denser.
